canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, UniGeo is characterized as a dataset for unified geometry problem solving. It is consistently framed as a subtask included within the larger MathVista benchmark. The dataset is used to evaluate models on geometry problems, specifically in the context of multiple-choice questions. The source material situates UniGeo alongside other geometry-focused datasets like GeoQA+, Geometry3K, and GEOS, positioning it within a collection of resources for this domain.
papers: G-LLaVA: Solving Geometric Problem with Multi-Modal Large Language Model
GeoX: Geometric Problem Solving Through Unified Formalized Vision-Language Pre-training
Is Your Model Really A Good Math Reasoner? Evaluating Mathematical Reasoning with Checklist
